Notes: Only difficulty is available as a signal. Minimal but valid.
Same-cat tiles are the ideal context for naming — they define what the category contains.
Cross-cat context has nothing to contribute to a name suggestion when no tile target is present.
Message: A category name can only use tiles from the same category.

Valid: SWITCH to S2 on submit attempt
Notes: Cross-cat context has nothing to contribute since there is no tile target. Drop T(B) on submit and keep same-cat context. Proceed as S2.
UI Behavior: Auto-deselect T(B) on submit, shake animation on deselected fields.
Message: Removed [Cat B] tiles — category names can only use tiles from the same category.

Notes: Only one name can be selected at a time. Selecting any name always deselects the previous one.
UI Behavior: Radio behavior: selecting any name deselects the previous. Brief visual flash on deselected name.
Message: Only one category name can be selected at a time.

Valid: SWITCH to S20 on submit if N(A) empty, else TRUE
Notes: No context at all. If the category name is also empty escalate to S20 — one submission is better than two.
UI Behavior: If N(A) is empty, visibly snap N(A) into selected state before submitting.
Message: Your category name will also be suggested.

Valid: SWITCH to S21 on submit if N(A) empty, else TRUE
Notes: Same-cat filled tiles provide context. If the category name is also empty escalate to S21 — suggest everything in one go.
UI Behavior: If N(A) is empty, visibly snap N(A) into selected state before submitting.
Message: Your category name will also be suggested.

Notes: Category name is filled so it serves as context not a target.
Richest possible same-cat context — all available signals for this category are present.
Cross-cat tiles are valid because tile targets are present. Used as red herring seeds.
Cross-cat name is valid because tile targets are present. The category name suggests a theme to play against.
Richest single red herring context — both the theme name and example tiles from another category.
Same-cat tiles anchor the suggestion to the right category; cross-cat tiles seed the red herring.
Multiple cross-cat contexts. AI should find a tile that could plausibly belong to more than one other category.
No context. AI must suggest tiles that could be confused for each other with no other signals.
Each target has same-cat context. AI can anchor mutual red herrings to each category's existing content.
Category names provide thematic anchors. AI knows what each category is about when choosing red herrings.
Third-category tiles add a third axis of confusion — AI should find tiles that could plausibly belong to any of the three.

Targets: T(A) empty + T(B) empty + T(C) empty
Notes: Maximum mutual red herring complexity across three categories.
No context available. AI suggests both the category name and tiles guided by difficulty only.
Filled tiles provide context for both the name suggestion and the empty tile targets.

Prompt Type: P6 variant - Co-suggest name and tile with red herring context
Notes: Cross-cat context is valid because tile targets are present. T(B) informs tile targets as red herrings; name suggestion uses same-cat context only.
Targets span two different categories with no logical relationship. A name for cat A and a tile for cat B cannot be meaningfully suggested together.
Message: A category name and tiles from different categories can't be suggested together — select one or the other.
